---
title: "Uncertainty_Indirect_Volatilisation_Biosolid_CA"
author: "Dr. Chih-Yu Hung"
date: "2026-04-30"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup}
# --- Packages ----
library(tidyverse)
library(future.apply)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE
)
```

```{r data_and_function}
# --- Data preparation ----
# Goal: uncertainty of indirect N2O from volatilised N due to biosolid N application.
# This analysis keeps only rows with biosolid N application and the selected inventory year.

target_year <- 2015

DF <- readRDS("Inputs/BiosolidN_corrected.rds") # Data from Chang's work

Data <- DF %>%
  filter(Inv_Year_ID == target_year) %>%
  select(
    Inv_Year_ID,
    Province_ID,
    Ecodistrict_ID,
    Fert_LCA_kg = BION_corrected
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    FRRC_GASM = 0.20,
    EFVD = 0.01
  ) %>%
  filter(Fert_LCA_kg > 0) %>%
  drop_na()

# --- Volatilisation N2O calculation ----
# FRRC_GASM is the fraction of applied biosolid N volatilised as NH3 and NOx.
# EFVD is the indirect N2O emission factor for volatilised N.
# This function returns province level totals and implied emission factors for each MC draw.

calc_vol <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    mutate(
      N_vol = Fert_LCA_kg * FRAC_GASM,
      N2O = N_vol * EFVD
    ) %>%
    group_by(Province_ID) %>%
    summarise(
      Tot.Fert = sum(Fert_LCA_kg, na.rm = TRUE),
      Tot.Nvol = sum(N_vol, na.rm = TRUE),
      Tot.N2O  = sum(N2O, na.rm = TRUE),
      N2O.IEF  = if_else(Tot.Fert > 0, Tot.N2O / Tot.Fert, NA_real_),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
}
```

```{r mc_settings}
# --- User knobs ----
# n_mc can be increased after testing the script with a smaller number.
# On Windows, keep workers modest to avoid memory pressure.

use_vec    <- requireNamespace("vctrs", quietly = TRUE)
n_mc       <- 5000
seed       <- 2026
workers    <- 4
batch_size <- 25
set.seed(seed)

# --- Random draw helpers ----
# p95 is the fractional half width of the 95 percent confidence interval.
# For example, p95 = 0.10 means mean plus or minus 10 percent as a 95 percent interval.

rnorm_ci <- function(mu, p95) {
  sd <- abs(p95) * abs(mu) / 1.96
  rnorm(length(mu), mean = mu, sd = sd)
}

# Draws n values from a triangular distribution with minimum a, mode c, and maximum b.
rtri1 <- function(n, a, c, b) {
  stopifnot(a <= c, c <= b, a < b)
  u <- runif(n)
  Fc <- (c - a) / (b - a)
  x <- ifelse(
    u < Fc,
    a + sqrt(u * (b - a) * (c - a)),
    b - sqrt((1 - u) * (b - a) * (b - c))
  )
  pmax(a, pmin(x, b))
}
```

```{r mc_worker}
# --- Core per iteration worker ----
# Each iteration perturbs biosolid N and model parameters, then recalculates volatilised N2O.
# Biosolid N uncertainty combines biosolid amount uncertainty and N content uncertainty by error propagation.

run_one_mc <- function(i, Data,
                       ci_list = list(
                         Fert_LCA_kg = sqrt(0.15^2 + 0.46^2)
                       ),
                       par_means = list(
                         FRAC_min  = 0.05 ,
                         FRAC_mode = 0.20 ,
                         FRAC_max  = 0.50 ,
                         EFVD_min  = 0.002,
                         EFVD_mode = 0.010,
                         EFVD_max  = 0.040
                       )) {
  df <- Data
  message("iteration = ", i)
  
  # --- 1) Stochastic perturbation of input data ----
  df <- df %>%
    mutate(
      Fert_LCA_kg = pmax(rnorm_ci(Fert_LCA_kg, ci_list$Fert_LCA_kg), 0),
      )
  
  # --- 2) Stochastic draw of FRAC_GASM and EFVD ----
  # Both are sampled once per MC iteration and applied to all ecodistricts.
  FRAC <- rtri1(
    1,
    a = par_means$FRAC_min,
    c = par_means$FRAC_mode,
    b = par_means$FRAC_max
  )
  
  EFVD <- rtri1(
    1,
    a = par_means$EFVD_min,
    c = par_means$EFVD_mode,
    b = par_means$EFVD_max
  )
  
  df <- df %>% mutate(FRAC_GASM = FRAC,
                      EFVD = EFVD)
  
  # --- 3) Run volatilisation calculation ----
  Result_Prov_MC <- calc_vol(df) %>%
    mutate(
      MC = i,
      EFVD_draw = EFVD,
      FRRC_GASM_mean = mean(df$FRRC_GASM, na.rm = TRUE)
    )
  
  Result_Prov_MC
}

# --- Batch worker ----
# Data is serialized once per batch instead of once per MC iteration.

run_batch <- function(ii, Data) {
  acc <- NULL
  
  for (i in ii) {
    res_i <- run_one_mc(i, Data = Data)
    acc <- if (is.null(acc)) res_i else {
      if (use_vec) vctrs::vec_rbind(acc, res_i) else bind_rows(acc, res_i)
    }
    rm(res_i)
  }
  
  gc(FALSE)
  acc
}
```

```{r run_mc}
# --- Run Monte Carlo simulation ----
# The result is province level volatilised N2O for each MC iteration.

future::plan(future::multisession, workers = workers)
# options(future.globals.maxSize = 2 * 1024^3) # Uncomment if the globals are too large.

idx <- seq_len(n_mc)
batches <- split(idx, ceiling(seq_along(idx) / batch_size))

syst_s <- Sys.time()

parts <- future_lapply(
  batches,
  run_batch,
  Data = Data,
  future.seed = TRUE
)

syst_f <- Sys.time()
syst_f - syst_s

Result_Prov_MC <- if (use_vec) vctrs::vec_rbind(!!!parts) else bind_rows(parts)
rm(parts)
gc(FALSE)

summary(Result_Prov_MC) #N2O, 2380.91, 4478.36
saveRDS(Result_Prov_MC, "Outputs/Uncertainty_Indirect_Volatilisation_Biosolid_Province_CA.rds")
```

```{r national_summary}
# --- National uncertainty summary ----
# N2O is converted to CO2e using molecular weight conversion and GWP100 = 265.

Result_MC <- Result_Prov_MC %>%
  group_by(MC) %>%
  summarise(
    N2O = sum(Tot.N2O, na.rm = TRUE),
    Tot.Fert = sum(Tot.Fert, na.rm = TRUE),
    Tot.Nvol = sum(Tot.Nvol, na.rm = TRUE),
    N2O.IEF = if_else(Tot.Fert > 0, N2O / Tot.Fert, NA_real_),
    EFVD_draw = first(EFVD_draw),
    FRRC_GASM_mean = first(FRRC_GASM_mean),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    CO2e = N2O * 44 / 28 * 265,
    `CO2e(kt)` = CO2e / 10^6
  )

summary(Result_MC) #CO2ekt 16.305, 16.784
saveRDS(Result_MC, "Outputs/Uncertainty_Indirect_Volatilisation_Biosolid_National_CA.rds")

CI <- Result_MC %>%
  summarise(
    median = median(`CO2e(kt)`, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean   = mean(`CO2e(kt)`, na.rm = TRUE),
    lower  = quantile(`CO2e(kt)`, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    upper  = quantile(`CO2e(kt)`, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
    rel_lower = (lower - median) / median * 100,
    rel_upper = (upper - median) / median * 100,
    rel_range = (upper - lower) / median * 100
  )

CI #- 63% + 80%
write.csv(CI, "Outputs/Uncertainty_Range_Indirect_Volatilisation_Biosolid_CA.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r province_summary}
# --- Province level uncertainty summary ----
# This table shows the median and 95 percent uncertainty interval by province.

CI_Prov <- Result_Prov_MC %>%
  mutate(
    CO2e = Tot.N2O * 44 / 28 * 265,
    `CO2e(kt)` = CO2e / 10^6
  ) %>%
  group_by(Province_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    median = median(`CO2e(kt)`, na.rm = TRUE),
    lower  = quantile(`CO2e(kt)`, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    upper  = quantile(`CO2e(kt)`, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
    rel_lower = (lower - median) / median * 100,
    rel_upper = (upper - median) / median * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(median))

CI_Prov #QC has the highest uncertainty. -64% + 88%
saveRDS(CI_Prov, "Outputs/Uncertainty_Indirect_Volatilisation_Biosolid_Province_CI_CA.rds")
```

```{r plot_distribution}
# --- Plot national uncertainty distribution ----
# The dashed vertical line is the median national indirect volatilisation emission estimate.

p1 <- ggplot(Result_MC, aes(x = `CO2e(kt)`)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40, color = "white") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = CI$median, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    x = expression(N[2]*O~CO[2]*e~"(kt)"),
    y = "Monte Carlo count",
    title = "Uncertainty of indirect N2O from biosolid N volatilisation in Canada"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

p1

ggsave(
  "Outputs/Uncertainty_Indirect_Volatilisation_Biosolid_CA.png",
  p1,
  width = 7,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 300
)
```
